---
title: "Advanced Pathway Schedule: Summer 2026"
---

## Summer Session

Location: Ponderosa High School, 2884 N. Steves Blvd, Flagstaff, AZ

Dates: July 8-10, 2026

Time: 8:00 AM - 4:00 PM

The advanced pathway follows the same start and end time as the introductory pathway. Participants will join the shared reimbursement and program logistics meeting, then split into pathway groups.

## Day 1: Python Foundations

| Time | Activity |
|---|---|
| 8:00-8:45 | Shared reimbursement meeting and program logistics |
| 8:45-9:10 | Advanced pathway welcome: three-day plan, instructors, participant introductions |
| 9:10-10:15 | Python warm-up: variables, functions, lists, arrays, and plotting |
| 10:15-10:30 | Break |
| 10:30-11:45 | Loops, model updates, and simulation |
| 11:45-1:45 | Lunch |
| 1:45-2:15 | Randomness, probability, and transition matrices |
| 2:15-2:30 | Break |
| 2:30-3:45 | Likelihood, simulation, and Monte Carlo intuition |
| 3:45-4:00 | Reflection and Day 2 preview |

## Day 2: Epidemic Models and Markov Chains

| Time | Activity |
|---|---|
| 8:00-8:15 | Review of Day 1 ideas and project goals |
| 8:15-10:15 | SIR models and Euler's method |
| 10:15-10:30 | Break |
| 10:30-12:00 | Simulation experiments and interpretation |
| 12:00-1:00 | Lunch |
| 1:00-2:15 | Markov chain states and transition matrices |
| 2:15-2:30 | Break |
| 2:30-3:45 | Classroom adaptation planning |
| 3:45-4:00 | Reflection and Day 3 preview |

## Day 3: MCMC and Inference

| Time | Activity |
|---|---|
| 8:00-8:20 | Review: simulation, probability, and likelihood |
| 8:20-10:15 | Bayesian inference and noisy observations |
| 10:15-10:30 | Break |
| 10:30-12:00 | MCMC intuition and implementation |
| 12:00-1:00 | Lunch |
| 1:00-3:00 | DataJam and classroom connections |
| 3:00-4:00 | Wrap-up, feedback, and next steps |
